channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Prepared tree pit with no tree planted, 14th April
item.description:
This tree pit has been prepared with a supporting post for a long period. Are there any plans to use it?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ridgeway Drive, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing slats from public benches, 14th April
item.description:
Missing slats from benches Been like this for a long time The only off-street seating area away from traffic/noise
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Banks Lane, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Number 95 have made their own make shift drive., 14th April
item.description:
The people living at number 95 have decided to chip away the pavement and get white lines painted Infront of their house to make their own make shift drive in the front garden. As far as I'm aware this isn't allowed as the road is too small for cars to be parked in the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chichester Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All along Windermere Road, 14th April
item.description:
There are pot holes all the way along Windermere Road and a very large one between 2-4 Windermere Road. The road has not been resurfaced for over 24 years. Also, all the paving stones are a cracked all the way along. Please can this be looked into.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Windermere Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on side of our building, 14th April
item.description:
Hi, Please can this be removed, we are leasing this building off hackney council. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Old Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household rubbish left outside the park, 14th April
item.description:
Some household rubbish dumped outside Butterfield Green
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nevill Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: House fence has collapsed blocking pavement, 14th April
item.description:
House fence has collapsed blocking pavement. This has been on going for over a year and nothing has been done
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25d, Cintra Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shelves and storage boxes dumped, 14th April
item.description:
Items have been in the same spot for over a week. roving council bulk waste van has stopped and *looked at it* twice - (Sat and Mon) am and just left it, while picking up other waste dumped on the opposite corner AND from the grounds of the block. Why just leave it?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Kennington Park Place, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned FULL dustbin, 14th April
item.description:
An abandoned dustbin which is in between two houses and is full up with waste and is blocking the road . It has been here for over 4weeks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 28, Cintra Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Red tent in grassy mound, 14th April
item.description:
Reported on Good Friday. Advised last Thursday that park wardens had spoken to occupants who were going to leave that day. Tent and occupants are still there this morning.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bayliss Avenue, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned van parked in road with very offensive language written upon it, 14th April
item.description:
This van (347 UHT is the visible registration number) has been abandoned on this particular road for at least 6 months. It has the words "FUCK ULEZ" written in large lettering on the rear which is very offensive.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 67, Belmont Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: E- Lime hire Bikes parked on Pavement, 14th April
item.description:
E- Lime hire Bikes parked on overcrowd Parking bay on Pavement Haymarket Caffe Concerto.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35,35a, Haymarket, Covent Garden, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree desperately needs pruning, 14th April
item.description:
The tree at the end of 134 Murray Avenue drive is very overhanging. I reported this last year and it’s now worse. The size of the branches dropping are dangerous to the general public as well as ourselves. They’re also causing damage to mine and the neighbours vehicles. The tree is also blocking a lot of light into our property as well as shedding which is coming inside our house, even when the window is only slightly open.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Murray Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: As ever the post office is a state, 14th April
item.description:
Rubbish everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mount Pleasant Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 14th April
item.description:
Alot of rubbish seen in the car park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clarence Yard, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped mattress, 14th April
item.description:
Someone has fly tipped their mattress on the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sandringham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste building at rear of property. that does not have any bins, 14th April
item.description:
Landlord or Tenant is disposing of waste illegally as there are no commercial bins on site. No waste contractors collecting rubbish from site. Rubbish is probably being fly tipped / dumped in the area Location 3 Birkbeck Mews

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Birkbeck Mews, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter and boxes by bin on pavement, 14th April
item.description:
Litter and boxes by bin on pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 94-140, Westmoreland Road, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dining chair dumped on pavement, 14th April
item.description:
It looks unsightly why do people think they can dump there unwanted furniture on the street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Somerhill Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Address the problem I reported for ticket number 9202165 the day before yesterday, 14th April
item.description:
Yes, let me tell you 'what is wrong': You, the Council are WRONG becauseI reported the above problem (ticket 9202165) the other day. You have written on this platform that it is 'sorted' and its NOT SORTED. None of it is sorted: your very badly designed platform does not allow a paying resident to itemise loads of issues IN EXACTLY THE SAME AREA unless they spent ages on your platform. PEOPLE DO NOT HAVE UNLIMITED TIME to deal with what should be the COUNCIL'S DUTY OF CARE. The area that I have called into question is right next door to my home. The building next door to my home is YOUR COUNCIL RESIDENT block - St David's Mansions. Your residents are people who have no investment in their home and so don't care a fig about their surround. That is the first problem. So it means NO-ONE from the block will be taking time out of their life to report the problem - overflowing bin aligning the block. A blue bin with no lid, meaning passers by are very attracted to the area as a very good fly tipping area. Plus the bins attract birds to take discarded food out of the bin, spread all over the pavement - then you have your rodent and fox fouling problem. Second problem within the same few yards, right next door to St David's Mansions aligninig Dawsons Hill: THE FLY TIPPING SITE. Its been a tip now for around 2 weeks and is getting BIGGER AND BIGGER. Third problem is the graffiti scrawled all over the cement side wall of St David's Mansions which faces my home. Again I reported this before Easter to MUSTAFA SEMANDA who has done absolutely NOTHING ABOUT IT. And now I find that you are actually posting stuff on your platform to indicate you have dealt with all of this. UNBELIEVABLE! I mean, really, what is it you peopoe at the Council are on? It's like every team member is completely spaced out because they don't see anything, they have no energy and they don't care. You need to get someone in charge to please contact me VERY URGENTLY.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 167, Dunstan's Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
